---
title: "Physics Beyond the Standard Model"
subtitle: "Summary of the course PHYS-F469"
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal" collapse="true"}
## ⚠️ Warning regarding these notes
The notes published on this website are based on my personal understanding of the material and have not been independently verified. While I hope they are helpful, there may be errors or inaccuracies. If you find any errors or have suggestions for improvement, please feel free to contact me: [a.d@csic.es](mailto:a.d@csic.es).
:::

**Teacher:** Michel TYTGAT & Steven LOWETTE (Year 2024-2025)  
**Official resources:** [<i class="bi bi-link-45deg"></i> ULB page](https://www.ulb.be/fr/programme/phys-f469){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-sm .ms-2}
[<i class="bi bi-folder2-open"></i> Dochub space](https://dochub.be/catalog/course/phys-f469){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-sm .ms-2}

---

## Theoretical Part

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 1: Basic Elementary Fields
![](./assets/BSMt/BSM - CH1.jpg){.rounded .mb-3 style="width: 100%; height: auto;"}

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 1 Notes](./assets/BSMt/BSMt - CH1.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 2: Standard Model
![](./assets/BSMt/BSM - CH2.jpg){.rounded .mb-3 style="width: 100%; height: auto;"}

* **2.1 Gauge symmetries**
* **2.2 EE-W interaction for leptons and quarks**
* **2.3 QCD interaction for quarks**
* **2.4 Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking of $SU(2)_L \times U(1)_Y$**
* **2.5 Fermion masses**
* **2.6 Some examples**
* **2.7 Limitations of the Standard Model**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 2 Notes](./assets/BSMt/BSMt - CH2.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 3: Chiral Fermions
![](./assets/BSMt/BSM - CH3.jpg){.rounded .mb-3 style="width: 100%; height: auto;"}

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 3 Notes](./assets/BSMt/BSMt - CH3.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 4: Neutrino Masses
![](./assets/BSMt/BSM - CH4.jpg){.rounded .mb-3 style="width: 100%; height: auto;"}

* **4.1 Standard model extension**
* **4.2 Experimental situations**
* **4.3 Effective field theory**
* **4.4 Path integral formalism**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 4 Notes](./assets/BSMt/BSMt - CH4.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 5: Grand Unification
![](./assets/BSMt/BSM - CH5.jpg){.rounded .mb-3 style="width: 100%; height: auto;"}

* **5.1 Running of $\lambda \phi^4$**
* **5.2 Yang - Mills theory**
* **5.3 Grand Unified Theories**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 5 Notes](./assets/BSMt/BSMt - CH5.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

:::

---

## Experimental Part

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 1: Beyond the Standard Model

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 1 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH1.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 2: Experimental Anomalies

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 2 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH2.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 3: Theoretical Landscape

* **3.1 Theoretical constraints on $m_H$**
* **3.2 Higgs properties**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 3 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH3.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 4: Higgs Property Measurements

* **4.1 Standard model extension**
* **4.2 Experimental situations**
* **4.3 Effective field theory**
* **4.4 Path integral formalism**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 4 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH4.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 5: Effective Field Theories

* **5.1 EFT in measurements**
* **5.2 EFT in searches**
* **5.3 Flavor physics**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 5 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH5.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 6: Dark Matter

* **6.1 Searches for dark matter**

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Chapter 6 Notes](./assets/BSMx/BSMx - CH6.pdf){.btn-surface .c-teal .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 7: Neutrinos

* **7.1 Neutrino mass**
* **7.2 Neutrino oscillations**
* **7.3 Theory of $m_\nu$**
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Chapter 8: Supersymmetry

* **8.1 Hierarchy problem**
* **8.2 Constructing supersymmetry**
* **8.3 Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model**
:::
:::

:::
